samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>An asynchronous FIFO implemented using dual-port RAM and additional logic for managing read and write pointers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Used to trigger write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Used to trigger read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of the FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of the FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Define parameters
        parameter WIDTH = 8;
        parameter DEPTH = 16;
        
        // Declare internal signals
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM_MEM[0:DEPTH-1];
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_reg;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_reg;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_next;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_next;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_out;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_in;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_in;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_out;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_out;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_next;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_next;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_reg;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_reg;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_out;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_in;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_in;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] wdata_out;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata_out;

[COMPILE verilator+cpp]
%Warning-DECLFILENAME: asyn_fifo.v:62:8: Filename 'asyn_fifo' does not match MODULE name: 'dual_port_RAM'
   62 | module dual_port_RAM #(parameter DEPTH = 16,  parameter WIDTH = 8)
      |        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
                       ... Use "/* verilator lint_off DECLFILENAME */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTH: asyn_fifo.v:36:81: Operator EQ expects 3 bits on the RHS, but RHS's SEL generates 2 bits.
                                 : ... In instance asyn_fifo
   36 | assign wfull = (wptr[ADDR_WIDTH] ^ wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1]) && (wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0] == wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-3:0]);
      |                                                                                 ^~
%Warning-UNDRIVEN: asyn_fifo.v:19:22: Signal is not driven: 'waddr'
                                    : ... In instance asyn_fifo
   19 | reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr, raddr;
      |                      ^~~~~
%Warning-UNDRIVEN: asyn_fifo.v:19:29: Signal is not driven: 'raddr'
                                    : ... In instance asyn_fifo
   19 | reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr, raddr;
      |                             ^~~~~
%Warning-UNUSED: asyn_fifo.v:39:5: Signal is not used: 'renc'
                                 : ... In instance asyn_fifo
   39 | reg renc = 0;
      |     ^~~~
%Warning-UNUSED: asyn_fifo.v:48:36: Signal is not used: 'rptr_sync2'

                                  : ... In instance asyn_fifo
